target.title: Fix OpenClaw command tool runtime path
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

## User Problem

`programs.openclaw.runtimePackages` is a Nix option for declaring command-line tools that OpenClaw should be able to use.

Before this PR, nix-openclaw made those packages available through an internal Codex-home profile path:

```text
agents/<id>/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin
```

That path was the wrong public contract. It coupled a general OpenClaw command-tool option to a Codex-specific filesystem layout.

## What This Changes

This PR makes `runtimePackages` mean one thing in nix-openclaw:

```text
Nix-managed command tools for OpenClaw instances.
```

It does that by:

- adding runtime package bin paths to the generated gateway wrapper `PATH`;
- rendering the same bin paths into global `tools.exec.pathPrepend`;
- prefixing agent-level `tools.exec.pathPrepend` only for agents that already override it, matching OpenClaw's agent-over-global precedence;
- using `lib.getBin` for package bin directories;
- removing the legacy Codex-home profile linker.

## Non-Goals

This PR deliberately does **not**:

- enable the Codex plugin;
- import the Codex runtime plugin for this check;
- export `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS`;
- export `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN`;
- configure Codex app-server launch arguments;
- prove native Codex `command/exec`, `thread/start`, `thread/resume`, or turn execution.

The OpenClaw product follow-up is separate: OpenClaw's Codex adapter should consume the existing `tools.exec.pathPrepend` config and project it into native Codex thread config. That belongs in `openclaw/openclaw`, not in nix-openclaw and not as a new Nix knob.

## Compatibility

User config stays the same:

```nix
programs.openclaw.runtimePackages = [ pkgs.jq ];
```

The compatibility break is limited to the old internal Codex-home profile path. Pre-1.0 deployments that depended on `agents/<id>/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin` should depend on `runtimePackages` / generated `tools.exec.pathPrepend` instead.

This PR does not clean up existing stale symlinks at the old path. That is an intentional maintainer decision point, not hidden behavior.

## Proof Map

This is the proof contract for the narrowed nix-openclaw change. CI green is not the proof by itself; the checks below are proof because they assert specific behavior and fail on the wrong state.

| Claim | Evaluator | What Fails On The Wrong State |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `runtimePackages` render into generated `tools.exec.pathPrepend`. | `nix/checks/openclaw-runtime-path.nix` evaluates Home Manager config with `runtimePackages = [ pkgs.hello ]`. | Throws if the generated config does not contain the `hello` bin directory. |
| Generated `pathPrepend` resolves the runtime package command. | `nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-path-smoke.mjs` builds a PATH from generated `pathPrepend` and runs `command -v hello && hello`. | Throws if `hello` resolves anywhere other than the expected Nix store bin path or does not run. |
| The gateway wrapper prepends the same runtime tool path. | The smoke executes the generated wrapper with `bash -x` and a base PATH that excludes the runtime package. | Throws if the wrapper trace does not include the expected `hello` bin directory. |
| User global and agent-specific `pathPrepend` entries are preserved after Nix runtime tool paths. | `openclaw-runtime-path.nix` configures `/custom/global` and `/custom/agent`. | Throws if either custom entry disappears, or if the runtime package bin directory is not before the custom entry. |
| Agents that inherit global exec config are not given a synthetic agent-level `pathPrepend`. | `openclaw-runtime-path.nix` includes an agent with `tools.exec.security` but no `tools.exec.pathPrepend`. | Throws if nix-openclaw creates an agent-level `pathPrepend` for that agent. |
| `runtimePackages` do not enable Codex. | `openclaw-runtime-path.nix` inspects generated `plugins.entries`. | Throws if `plugins.entries.codex` appears. |
| `runtimePackages` do not configure Codex app-server launch environment. | `openclaw-runtime-path.nix`, `openclaw-runtime-path-smoke.mjs`, Linux activation, and macOS activation inspect service/wrapper output. | Throws or fails if `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS` or `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN` appears. |
| The old Codex-home runtime profile activation is gone. | `openclaw-runtime-path.nix` inspects Home Manager activation text; the tracked linker script is deleted. | Throws if old activation names or linker script names appear. |
| Platform activation sees runtimePackages, not only built-in tools. | Linux and macOS activation fixtures now use `pkgs.hello`, which is not in the built-in OpenClaw toolset. | Fails if generated wrapper/config do not contain `hello-*/bin`. |

## CI Proof

`flake.nix` includes `runtime-path` inside `checks.<system>.runtime-smoke`.

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` builds `runtime-smoke` in both supported-surface jobs:

- `Linux supported surface`: `.#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-smoke`
- `macOS supported surface`: `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-smoke`

The same workflow builds platform activation on Linux and builds/runs the macOS launchd activation smoke.

Current pushed head: `af74418962f9b653d9e7b26fcfe48a68a65072c5`.

GitHub CI on that head is green for:

- Flake input provenance
- Linux supported surface
- macOS supported surface

Garnix is also green for flake evaluation and package/check builds.

## Local Behavior Proof

On the clean committed tree for `af74418962f9b653d9e7b26fcfe48a68a65072c5`, a forced rebuild of `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-path` printed:

```text
openclaw-runtime-path proof:
- generated tools.exec.pathPrepend resolves /nix/store/...-hello-2.12.2/bin/hello
- gateway wrapper prepends the same runtime tool path
- runtimePackages do not configure Codex app-server launch argv
```

The local macOS activation smoke also passed against a freshly built `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.platform-activation` out-link, including the `hello-*/bin` wrapper/config assertions.

## Remaining Human Decision

The remaining maintainer decision is compatibility: accept that pre-1.0 nix-openclaw stops managing the internal Codex-home profile path and does not clean stale symlinks there, or ask for a separate cleanup/migration behavior before merge.
target.author: joshp123
